title: picea lambertiana
intro: Picea lambertiana, also known as the red fir or giant fir, is a species of coniferous tree native to the western coast of North America. The essential oil extracted from its needles possesses antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it valuable in traditional medicine for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Picea lambertiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Picea lambertiana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to support immune function, relieve respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being.
